sections: HCM-0.4 crate ownership and dependency contract

repo_truth: The worktree contains a three-file HCM-0.5 planning subject and two immutable planning review dispatches on feat/handbook-contract-membrane at baseline HEAD 6cef8b952786c768b65d5da5d6cf3562a1f59b7c. HCM-0.4 is completed dependency evidence; HCM-0.9 is abandoned and is neither resume nor topology authority. The monolithic 05 file remains canonical, its HCM-0.5 sections are preliminary, and no contract/dock runtime exists. This is a fresh read-only complete-subject planning review; judge only the exact current manifest and named authority.
allowed_scope: Read and replay the exact three-file subject manifest and the named authority sections.
Run read-only Markdown, schema/example, link, portability, Git, and handoff checks needed to validate the plan.
Review whether the packet is independently executable and sufficiently exact for the parent to apply the HCM-0.5 design freeze after CLEAN.
non_goals: No repository edits, commits, new dispatches, global handoff, ledger write, or user task hop by the reviewer.
No Rust/runtime work, HCM-0.6 default selection, HCM-0.9 repair/resume, contract-catalog decomposition, leaf files, or runtime proof-gate promotion.
Do not treat HCM-0.4 completion or the existing preliminary HCM-0.5 prose as proof that the proposed semantics are correct.
tasks: Replay the manifest and confirm the review subject is exactly the three HCM-0.5 planning files.

Review completeness against the user objective: exact questions, owners, lifecycle/state machines, request/result/evidence, negative/refusal cases, security/isolation, first proof-dock selection, affected 01-06 sections, proof wall, non-goals, review budget, and stop behavior.

Review semantic correctness: separate contract lifecycle from evaluation; exact claim applicability; evidence identity/provenance/freshness/cardinality/Resolution; closed verdict meanings; hard-fail gate composition; validator-as-witness boundary.

Review process protocol for executable clarity and security: manifest negotiation, one-document JSON framing, default-deny grants, path/output safety, network, timeouts, cancellation, process-tree cleanup, refusal/failure, and no partial evidence.
Review the JSON Schema first-proof-dock candidate comparison and decide whether the selection is evidence-backed within HCM-0.5 authority or requires human/product authority.
Review ordinary operation additions for compatibility with frozen HCM-0.4 operation ownership, mutability, idempotency, write-set, DTO, transport, bridge, and publication contracts.
Review scope discipline: no HCM-0.2/0.3/0.4 semantic rewrite, HCM-0.6 selection, HCM-0.9 reuse, leaf files, universal validator, marketplace, runtime implementation, or proof promotion.
Review task order, independent executability, verification specificity, review-budget accounting, and honest stop conditions.

Return findings first ordered Critical, Required, Optional, Nit. Every actionable finding must include exact file/line, violated authority or objective, why it matters, smallest bounded remediation, and verification. Return CLEAN only if no actionable finding remains.
contracts_and_gates: 04 HCM-0.5 objective and exit gate; Phase 0 non-goals; Phase 5 HCM-5.1 through HCM-5.4 boundaries
01 invariants 1, 4, 5, 6, 8, 9, 20, and 21 plus the dock posture
05 frozen HCM-0.4 owner/operation/transport contracts and preliminary HCM-0.5 sections
06 open PG-CONTRACT-01, PG-DOCK-01, PG-GATE-01 and applicable regression rules
User review budget: four complete-subject submissions, three remediation rounds, no fifth review
stop_conditions: Return a Required finding if the packet would require product authority, broader scope, runtime proof, or redesign before canonical edits.
Return blocked if the exact subject/authority or required read-only capability is unavailable.
Do not modify repository state or write orchestration artifacts.
